description: v17 (2026-05-12): Phase LF.5 — added skipForBackends:[claude,codex,gemini] to wisdom-framework, frontend-skill-guidance, layout-reference, visual-design-rubric-pack and browser-qa-pack. CLI agents have MCP tools (read_component, mchatai-web-components MCP, file read on workdir symlinks) to pull these on demand. Front-loading ~70KB of reference content was burning their turn budget and pushing runs to maxTurns 'no parseable artifact' (ContextBudgetRun HARD_WARN 96828 chars, top3 wisdom-framework=43996 frontend-skill-guidance=15041 wisdom-universal=11996). HTTP-only backends (openai/anthropic-direct) still receive everything. v16 (2026-05-05): Phase LF.1 — flipped web-components layer to inlineSource:false (trim mode). Drops ~30-50KB per miniApp prompt by emitting only the catalog index (id/summary/exports/path/contracts) instead of full component source. The LLM fetches source on demand via the mchatai-web-components MCP (preferred) or via direct Read of `./web-components/<path>` from the cwd. Companion changes: requiredComponents/optionalComponents split per recipe (LF.4), Swift WebComponentRecipe.enforcedComponents accessor, Node validator subset check. Wisdom rule u-031 instructs the LLM that the catalog index lists every component; read source from `./web-components/<path>` before importing — do not invent its API. Legacy mini-apps are self-contained HTML, but Phase SR.WEB Web Components Recipes may use bundled ES module sidecars under ./web-components/. v2 added active-memory layer (Phase MA.3). v3 added excludeTitlePatterns to drop CCAA / AgentRuntime / ScheduledJob log spam from retrieval results. v4 (2026-04-18): added wisdom-framework layer at priority 85 with its own 3500-token budget so framework-specific packs (card-games, arcade-games, visual-quality, etc.) get a dedicated slot instead of competing with universal rules in a single 2500-token pool. v5 (2026-04-19): bumped wisdom-framework to 11000 tokens (~44000 chars) because 3500 tokens was truncating card-games + visual-quality packs combined (30k raw → ~45k rendered) — rules late in the resolve order (cg-011, cg-012, cg-013, cg-020) were being chopped off at '…[truncated]' and the LLM never saw them. v6 (2026-04-19): added layout-reference layer at priority 80 — picks a curated HTML skeleton from assets/layout-references/ matching the goal (hearts/spades/bridge → trick-taking-hearts.html). LLMs follow concrete code patterns far more reliably than they follow 1200-char prose rules; the evaluator can also grep for preserved structural ids. Root cause of the Hearts 'rules visible but ignored' plateau: rules describe, references demonstrate. v7 (2026-04-20): Phase DF — pinned frontend-skill wisdom pack at priority 82 between wisdom-framework (85) and layout-reference (80). Unlike wisdom-framework which is keyword-gated, this pack always loads for miniApp turns so taste rules (hero budget, one-composition, no-cards-by-default, motion recipe, copy discipline) land even when the goal prose contains zero design keywords. v10 (2026-05-01): Phase SR.WEB initial web-components layer at priority 83. v11 (2026-05-01): widened the web-components slice to 10 components / 46KB after splitting broad puzzle/card recipes into more specific Lego recipes. v12 (2026-05-01): resolved legacy single-file guidance conflicts so Web Components Recipes are allowed to use bundled module imports. v13 (2026-05-03): Phase H2.2 — bumped web-components layer to 14 components / 60KB / 11K tokens because TD recipe needs 13 components incl. entities.entity transitive — was hitting the 10-component cap and silently dropping resources.vector-sprites. v14 (2026-05-03): bumped to 16/80K/14K because TD recipe was still hitting the cap and dropping resources.swatches + resources.audio-manager — runtime AudioManager-undefined errors persisted.
